we.label: WE (word emb)
we.caption: Static word embeddings — the model's input lookup table. The same word type maps to the SAME vector, so the cloud is tight duplicate clumps (one per token type): intrinsic dim ≈0, nn-cosine ≈0.93. This is a finite vocabulary, not a continuous cloud.

we_pe_tt.label: WE-all (+pos+type)
we_pe_tt.caption: WE + position + token-type — the actual input to layer 0. Adding the positional signal injects a strong shared direction (anisotropy jumps), collapsing participation ratio: every token now points partly the same way.

L0.label: L0
L0.caption: After the embedding LayerNorm. The shared positional component is normalised away, so participation ratio recovers — but tokens are still barely contextualised (near-duplicate word clumps remain).

L3.label: L3
L3.caption: Transformer layer 3 (early). Attention has mixed context across tokens; the word-lookup clumps blur into a contextual cloud (intrinsic dim 4.9, nn-cosine 0.923). Can local geometry still reproduce it?

L6.label: L6
L6.caption: Transformer layer 6 (early). Attention has mixed context across tokens; the word-lookup clumps blur into a contextual cloud (intrinsic dim 5.1, nn-cosine 0.898). Can local geometry still reproduce it?

L12.label: L12
L12.caption: Transformer layer 12 (middle). Attention has mixed context across tokens; the word-lookup clumps blur into a contextual cloud (intrinsic dim 5.3, nn-cosine 0.942). Can local geometry still reproduce it?

L18.label: L18
L18.caption: Transformer layer 18 (late). Attention has mixed context across tokens; the word-lookup clumps blur into a contextual cloud (intrinsic dim 4.7, nn-cosine 0.941). Can local geometry still reproduce it?

L24.label: L24
L24.caption: Transformer layer 24 (late). Attention has mixed context across tokens; the word-lookup clumps blur into a contextual cloud (intrinsic dim 5.4, nn-cosine 0.954). Can local geometry still reproduce it?

weall_full.label: WE-all (full vocab)
weall_full.caption: The FULL word-embedding matrix — every one of the 30,522 vocabulary tokens (model.embeddings.word_embeddings.weight), not a text sample. Genuinely distinct points, so this is the one stage we can measure over the whole token space. This is what the model's input dictionary looks like as a shape before any context.

weall64.label: WE-all [:64] (MRL)
weall64.caption: The full vocabulary, but Matryoshka-truncated to the first 64 of 1024 dimensions (MRL[:64]) and re-normalized. Keeping only the leading dimensions asks: how much of the dictionary's shape survives when you throw away 94% of the coordinates?
